transcripts.title: Certis Belchim x CultureMonkey - Reports Discussion

summary.overview:
- **Executive Report Format:** Team agreed on a structured format featuring survey overview, scores, and actionable recommendations.

- **Country-Level Data:** Decision made to include country and department-level data for clearer alignment with leadership responsibilities.

- **Report Timeline:** Final report due by April 17; seven working days turnaround after confirming content.

- **Dashboard Updates:** Previous survey data removed from dashboards for clarity; managers will access only the latest results.

- **Thematic Analysis:** People science team will provide a thematic analysis of feedback, enhancing insights alongside numeric data.

- **Comparative Data Inclusion:** Side-by-side comparison slides to display trends between current and previous surveys will be added for leadership.
summary.action_items:
**Domnita Damian**
Review the report generated from the CultureMonkey platform for the current survey results to confirm if it meets expectations (07:23)

**Magdalena Majkowska**
Confirm the slides format and detail requirements for the executive presentation, including populations (countries, global departments), key comparison data, and any design preferences, to give sufficient lead time before April 17th deadline (14:53)

**Karthik N**
Provide sample executive presentation format to Certis Belchim participants during the meeting (04:14)
Exclude the previous survey results from the manager dashboards for clearer access to current survey data and confirm when done (12:41)
Share the ENPS methodological slides with Certis Belchim for internal briefing and presentation use (18:33)
Initiate thematic analysis of textual feedback received from the survey, prepare a separate report for leaders, and begin work on thematic analysis immediately (18:33)
Coordinate with people science team to deliver the completed executive presentation slides within the timeline, incorporating requested customizations (18:33)
Keep Yugi informed of all discussed details and ensure he resumes communication with Certis Belchim from tomorrow (19:14)
summary.notes:
## **Leadership Presentation Format**

The team agreed on using a structured executive report format to present survey results to leadership.

- **Customizable executive report format introduced by Culture Monkey** includes survey overview, overall and demographic scores, ENPS details, and driver performance breakdown (04:14)
    - The report features question-wise top and bottom rankings and actionable recommendations from people science experts
    - Domnita explained last year’s presentation was manually created without platform-generated slides (03:39)
    - Karthik highlighted flexibility to add or remove sections based on leadership needs and provide editable slides

- **Decision to include country-level data for local populations and department-level data for global teams** to align with leadership responsibilities (06:38)
    - The current report format was considered exhaustive and similar to last year’s by Domnita
    - Magdalena emphasized the need to finalize which populations to include and potentially adjust presentation length for executives
    - Karthik noted question-level detail is usually reserved for HRBP or regional heads, not executives

- **Report generation timeline set to meet leadership meeting deadline on April 17** with a review by HR director Bianca beforehand (09:52)
    - Karthik committed to a delivery turnaround of about **seven working days** once final content is confirmed
    - Magdalena stressed the urgency given Easter holidays and short turnaround, requesting early start on slide generation
    - The team agreed to confirm exact slide contents soon to allow time for edits

## **Survey Data Access and Dashboard Configuration**

Adjustments were planned to simplify survey data visibility for managers and align dashboard content with current survey results.

- **Exclusion of previous survey data from main dashboards** to show only the latest survey results to managers and sub-admins (11:59)
    - Karthik confirmed this backend change would prevent confusion caused by displaying averaged results from multiple surveys
    - Individual reports for prior surveys will remain accessible separately for detailed review
    - Magdalena agreed this change would make tool usage easier for managers viewing team results

- **Clarification and support planned for interpreting ENPS scores** to improve understanding and communication across leadership (12:41)
    - Karthik noted availability of slides explaining ENPS calculation and methodology which can be included in presentations
    - Domnita expressed skepticism regarding ENPS usefulness, noting it was not used last year, but Magdalena said this may change based on HR director input
    - Final decision on ENPS inclusion will be confirmed after discussions with Bianca

## **Textual Feedback Analysis and Reporting**

The team planned advanced analysis of open-text survey comments to provide deeper insights beyond numeric scores.

- **People science team will deliver a thematic analysis report of qualitative feedback** using partially AI-driven and manually verified methods (15:38)
    - Karthik explained the AI component automates initial categorization but manual checks ensure accuracy and alignment with actual comments
    - The thematic analysis will be a separate report, typically 4-5 slides, distinct from the main executive presentation
    - Magdalena welcomed this service and confirmed no additional cost will be charged, as this is included in the existing survey package (16:31)

- **Thematic analysis intended to complement the numeric results and support leadership understanding** of key feedback themes and improvement areas
    - This approach reflects increased involvement by the Data Science and People Science teams to add value for enterprise clients
    - Karthik emphasized that providing comprehensive insights helps demonstrate the survey’s value to leadership and supports follow-up actions

## **Survey Comparison and Reporting Enhancements**

The leadership presentation will include comparative data to highlight trends and changes in survey results.

- **Inclusion of side-by-side comparison slides for key driver scores between current and previous surveys** planned to show trends clearly (17:15)
    - Karthik shared an example where each slide displays prior year scores next to current results with explanations of increases, decreases, or stability
    - This feature helps leadership quickly grasp progress or emerging issues over time
    - Magdalena confirmed this comparison would be valuable and requested it be included in the upcoming presentation

- **Final report format and slide selection to be confirmed via email for refinement and timely delivery** (18:33)
    - Karthik will send ENPS explanatory slides and coordinate exclusion of old survey data from dashboards immediately
    - The team agreed to finalize slide contents soon to allow time for adjustments before the leadership meeting deadline
    - Yogi will resume contact with Magdalena for follow-ups once available, ensuring smooth ongoing communication (19:14)

summary.overview:
- **Need for New Platform:** Aloha needs a structured pulse survey tool for **150 employees** and **40 contractors** to improve feedback.  
- **Culture Monkey Overview:** The platform supports multi-language surveys and rich analytics through various channels like Slack and email.  
- **Survey Features:** Includes templates, anonymity options, and detailed reports with participation rates and sentiment analysis.  
- **Pricing Structure:** Set at **INR 380,000 annually** for up to 150 employees, with no extra fees for features.  
- **Decision Process:** Evaluated by India and US leadership for commercial viability and improved actionable insights.  
- **Next Steps:** Ongoing support from Culture Monkey with follow-up on product details and onboarding assistance.
summary.action_items:
**Shashi Kanth Gangaboina**
Share existing survey questions with Vishnu for review and customization suggestions (02:43)
Provide feedback or queries after reviewing product and commercial details that Vishnu will send (20:59)

**Vishnu**
Review Shashi’s provided survey questions and assist with question customization (02:55)
Send detailed product presentation, commercial proposal, and contact information to Shashi post-meeting (20:59)
Support onboarding and survey launch process once the decision is made (20:44)
summary.notes:
## **Employee Engagement Platform Needs**

The company Aloha is seeking a structured pulse survey tool to improve employee feedback processes and analytics.

- **Unstructured feedback system triggers need for new platform** at Aloha with about **150 employees** and **40 contractors** (02:08)  
    - Current feedback is informal and lacks tracking and actionable insights  
    - Existing HRMS tools provide limited survey capabilities without follow-up actions  
    - Immediate launch target is within **one week**, indicating urgency  
    - Leadership wants expert recommendations for survey question length and structure

- **Aloha operates as a US-headquartered product company with India as development hub** (02:08)  
    - Remote work setup limits demographic filters for survey analysis  
    - Focus on department or role-level segmentation rather than detailed demographic splits  
    - Employee feedback needs to be anonymous for candid responses

## **Culture Monkey Platform Overview**

Culture Monkey offers a comprehensive employee engagement suite designed to close the loop between feedback and action.

- **Platform supports multi-language surveys, multiple distribution channels, and rich analytics** (04:24)  
    - Surveys can be sent via Slack, email, WhatsApp, MS Teams, and more  
    - Analytics include heat maps, demographic breakdowns, and qualitative feedback analysis  
    - Inbuilt chatbot allows querying insights and generating action plans automatically  
    - AI suggests action plans but allows manual customization and collaboration

- **Manager dashboards provide real-time engagement metrics and benchmarks** (13:49)  
    - Managers see engagement scores, ENPS trends, strengths/weaknesses, and industry comparisons  
    - Option to toggle visibility to skip managers with very few reportees for more meaningful insights  
    - Designed to reduce manual reporting and encourage proactive management

## **Survey Creation and Reporting Features**

The platform simplifies survey building and delivers detailed reporting to guide decision-making.

- **Survey setup includes template selection, multi-language support, and anonymous options** (06:51)  
    - Supports rating, single/multi-select, free text, and various rating scales  
    - Employee data upload for audience targeting with automated reminders  
    - Survey links do not require portal logins and support resuming incomplete responses

- **Post-survey, detailed reports show participation rates, ENPS scores, and driver-level insights** (08:49)  
    - Heat maps enable drill-down by team, location, manager, and other layers  
    - Qualitative themes summarized with positive and negative sentiment highlights  
    - Action plans generated automatically based on low-scoring drivers for quick response

## **Commercial and Decision-Making Context**

Pricing and evaluation are based on headcount with consideration for organizational scale and investment rationale.

- **Pricing set at INR 380,000 annually for up to 150 employees with unlimited surveys** (16:45)  
    - Includes dedicated account manager and full platform access without feature restrictions  
    - Pricing model is strictly per employee headcount, no additional fees for features  
    - Culture Monkey handles question framing, survey launches, and analytics support

- **Decision process involves India and US leadership teams evaluating commercial viability** (17:30)  
    - Current platform is minimal and lacks analytics and customization required for growth  
    - Previous tools used were SurveyMonkey, Aeon, Tower Watson, but lacked actionable insights  
    - Emphasis on balancing investment cost with the value of improved feedback and actionability

- **Aloha's goal is to enhance participation and generate meaningful, expert-driven insights** (19:35)  
    - Current offline discussions lack openness; platform aims to encourage honest feedback  
    - Culture Monkey’s analytics and action planning are key differentiators noted by Vishnu  
    - Implementation support offered to accelerate launch and ongoing survey management

## **Next Steps and Support Commitment**

Vishnu from Culture Monkey commits to ongoing support and providing detailed product and commercial information.

- **Post-meeting follow-up to share product details and commercial proposal** (21:08)  
    - Vishnu will email documentation and contact info for continuous engagement  
    - Open invitation for further discussions as India leadership evaluates options  
    - Emphasis on seamless onboarding and dedicated account management to ease transition
